task_completion.description: The diagram shows a 5-generation hierarchical family tree starting with King George V at the top, containing at least 20 shapes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the draw.io window, there should be a tree diagram with King George V at the root, branching down through 5 levels (generations). There should be approximately 20 or more shapes representing people.

task_completion.description: Shapes contain names and birth/death years, and deceased members are visually distinguished from living ones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check the text inside the shapes to ensure it includes names and years (e.g., '1865-1936'). Deceased members (like George V or Elizabeth II) must have a different fill color, border style, or visual marker compared to living members (like Prince William or Prince George).

task_completion.description: Solid lines are used for parent-child relationships and dashed lines for marriages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the connecting lines between shapes. Lines connecting spouses (horizontal relationships) must be dashed. Lines connecting parents to children (vertical/hierarchical relationships) must be solid.

task_completion.description: A second page titled 'Legend' is created to explain the styles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom of the draw.io interface for a page tab named 'Legend', or a screenshot showing the actual legend page that explains what the colors/borders and solid/dashed lines mean.

task_completion.description: The .drawio and .png files are saved to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Desktop background or a file manager window for the files 'windsor_family_tree.drawio' and 'windsor_family_tree.png'.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io application GUI to build the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io interface being actively used to drag shapes, type text, and connect lines, rather than just running a script in the terminal to generate the file.

integrity.description: The PNG file is a genuine export of the created diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PNG file should visually match the diagram created in the draw.io application, and not be an image downloaded from the internet.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The family tree must start with King George V (1865-1936). The 5 generations include: Gen 1: George V; Gen 2: George VI, Edward VIII; Gen 3: Elizabeth II (1926-2022); Gen 4: Charles III, Diana, William, Harry; Gen 5: George (2013), Charlotte (2015), Louis (2018), Archie (2019). Deceased members include George V, Mary of Teck, George VI, Elizabeth II, Prince Philip, Princess Diana. Living members include Charles III, William, Harry, Catherine, Meghan, George, Charlotte. The diagram must have at least 20 shapes and 15 edges. Solid lines = parent-child, Dashed lines = marriage.
